chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: The hard assumption here is that you've watched part 3, giving an intuitive walkthrough of the

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: backpropagation algorithm. Here we get a little bit more formal and dive into the relevant calculus.

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: It's normal for this to be at least a little confusing, so the mantra to regularly pause

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: and ponder certainly applies as much here as anywhere else. Our main goal is to show how

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: people in machine learning commonly think about the chain rule from calculus in the context of

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: networks, which has a different feel from how most introductory calculus courses approach the subject.

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: For those of you uncomfortable with the relevant calculus, I do have a whole series on the topic.

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: Let's start off with an extremely simple network, one where each layer has a single neuron in it.

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: So this particular network is determined by three weights and three biases, and our goal is to

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: understand how sensitive the cost function is to these variables. That way we know which

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: adjustments to those terms is going to cause the most efficient decrease to the cost function.

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: And we're just going to focus on the connection between the last two neurons.

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: Let's label the activation of that last neuron with a superscript L, indicating which layer it's in.

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: So the activation of the previous neuron is a L-1. These are not exponents, they're just a way of

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: indexing what we're talking about, since I want to save subscripts for different indices later on.

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: Let's say that the value we want this last activation to be for a given training example is Y.

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: For example, Y might be 0 or 1. So the cost of this simple network for a single training example

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: is AL-Y squared. We'll denote the cost of that one training example as C0.

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: As a reminder, this last activation is determined by a weight, which I'm going to call WL,

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: times the previous neuron's activation, plus some bias, which I'll call BL.

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: And then you pump that through some special nonlinear function like the sigmoid or ReLU.

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: It's actually going to make things easier for us if we give a special name to this weighted sum,

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: like Z, with the same superscript as the relevant activations.

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: So this is a lot of terms, and a way that you might conceptualize it is that the weight,

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: the previous action, and the bias all together are used to compute Z, which in turn lets us compute

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: A, which finally, along with the constant Y, lets us compute the cost.

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: And of course, AL-1 is influenced by its own weight and bias and such.

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: But we're not going to focus on that right now.

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: Now all of these are just numbers, right? And it can be nice to think of each one as having

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: its own little number line. Our first goal is to understand how sensitive the cost function is

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: to small changes in our weight, WL. Or phrased differently, what is the derivative of C with respect to WL?

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: When you see this del W term, think of it as meaning some tiny nudge to W, like a change by 0.01,

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: and think of this del C term as meaning whatever the resulting nudge to the cost is. What we want

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: is their ratio. Conceptually, this tiny nudge to WL causes some nudge to ZL, which in turn causes some

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: nudge to AL, which directly influences the cost.

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: So we break things up by first looking at the ratio of a tiny change to ZL to this tiny change W,

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: that is, the derivative of ZL with respect to WL. Likewise, you then consider the ratio of the change

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: to AL to the tiny change in ZL that caused it, as well as the ratio between the final nudge to C,

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: and this intermediate nudge to AL. This right here is the chain rule, where multiplying together

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: these three ratios gives us the sensitivity of C to small changes in WL.

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: So on screen right now, there's a lot of symbols, and take a moment to make sure it's clear what

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: they all are. Because now, we're going to compute the relevant derivatives.

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: The derivative of C with respect to AL works out to be 2 times AL minus Y. Notice, this means that its size

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: is proportional to the difference between the network's output and the thing that we want it to be.

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: So if that output was very different, even slight changes stand to have a big impact on the final cost

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: function. The derivative of AL with respect to ZL is just the derivative of our sigmoid function,

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: or whatever non-linearity you choose to use. And the derivative of ZL with respect to WL,

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: in this case, comes out just to be AL minus 1. Now I don't know about you, but I think it's easy to

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: get stuck head down in the formulas without taking a moment to sit back and remind yourself of what they

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: all actually mean. In the case of this last derivative, the amount that that small nudge to the weight

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: influence the last layer depends on how strong the previous neuron is. Remember, this is where that

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: neurons that fire together wire together idea comes in. And all of this is the derivative with respect

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: to WL only of the cost for a specific single training example. Since the full cost function

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: involves averaging together all those costs across many different training examples, its derivative

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: requires averaging this expression that we found over all training examples. And of course that is

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: just one component of the gradient vector, which itself is built up from the partial derivatives of

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: the cost function with respect to all those weights and biases. But even though that's just one of the

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: many partial derivatives we need, it's more than 50% of the work. The sensitivity to the bias, for example,

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: is almost identical. We just need to change out this del z del w term for a del z del b.

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: And if you look at the relevant formula, that derivative comes out to be 1.

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: Also, and this is where the idea of propagating backwards comes in, you can see how sensitive this

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: cost function is to the activation of the previous layer. Namely, this initial derivative in the chain

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: rule expression, the sensitivity of z to the previous activation comes out to be the weight, WL.

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: And again, even though we're not going to be able to directly influence that previous layer activation,

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: it's helpful to keep track of, because now we can just keep iterating this same chain rule idea

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: backwards to see how sensitive the cost function is to previous weights and previous biases.

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: And you might think that this is an overly simple example, since all layers just have one neuron,

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: and that things are going to get exponentially more complicated for a real network.

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: But honestly, not that much changes when we give the layers multiple neurons.

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: Really, it's just a few more indices to keep track of.

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: Rather than the activation of a given layer simply being AL, it's also going to have a subscript,

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: indicating which neuron of that layer it is. Let's go ahead and use the letter K to index the layer

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: L-1, and J to index the layer L. For the cost, again we look at what the desired output is,

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: but this time we add up the squares of the differences between these last layer activations

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: and the desired output. That is, you take a sum over ALJ minus YJ squared.

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: Since there's a lot more weights, each one has to have a couple more indices to keep track of where it is,

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: so let's call the weight of the edge connecting this k-th neuron to the j-th neuron WLJK.

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: Those indices might feel a little backwards at first, but it lines up with how you'd index the

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: weight matrix that I talked about in the part 1 video. Just as before, it's still nice to give a

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: name to the relevant weighted sum, like Z, so that the activation of the last layer is just your

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: special function, like the sigmoid, applied to Z. You can kind of see what I mean, right? Where all of

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: these are essentially the same equations that we had before in the 1 neuron per layer case,

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: it's just that it looks a little more complicated. And indeed, the chain rule derivative expression

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: describing how sensitive the cost is to a specific weight looks essentially the same. I'll leave it

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: to you to pause and think about each of those terms if you want. What does change here, though,

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: is the derivative of the cost with respect to one of the activations in the layer L-1.

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: In this case, the difference is that the neuron influences the cost function through multiple

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: different paths. That is, on the one hand, it influences AL , which plays a role in the cost

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: function, and you have to add those up. And that, well, that's pretty much it. Once you know how

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: sensitive the cost function is to the activations in this second-to-last layer, you can just repeat

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: the process for all the weights and biases feeding into that layer. So pat yourself on the back! If all

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: of this makes sense, you have now looked deep into the heart of backpropagation, the workhorse behind

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: how neural networks learn. These chain rule expressions give you the derivatives that determine

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: each component in the gradient that helps minimize the cost of the network by repeatedly stepping downhill.

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: If you sit back and think about all that, this is a lot of layers of complexity to wrap your mind around,

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: so don't worry if it takes time for your mind to digest it all.

chunks.title: Backpropagation calculus Deep Learning
chunks.text: Thank you.
